issues_found: CRITICAL STAGNATION: ZERO EXPERIMENTS OR REVISIONS FOR 2 CONSECUTIVE ITERATIONS. The paper is byte-identical to iter_007. The score has been 6.5 for 3 consecutive reviews (iter 6, 7, 8). The system is consuming review cycles without making scientific progress. The 3 highest-value experiments (activation patching, tightened hedging, CMI at L0=22) have been recommended for 3 consecutive reviews and never executed. The writing-review loop is running without experimental input -- a self-perpetuating stagnation cycle.

ACTIVATION PATCHING ON 9 CORE WORDS UNEXECUTED (3rd consecutive review requesting). The ONLY metric-independent causal evidence for competitive exclusion. Resolves the central two-interpretation ambiguity: does JumpReLU genuinely lack absorption (interpretation a) or is the metric miscalibrated (interpretation b)? The 9 persistent core words have identified child features (e.g., feature_idx 6740 for 'eight', 5021 for 'lower'). Zeroing each child feature and checking parent recovery takes ~1 GPU-hour. Without this, the paper cannot make any causal claim. This has been the highest-information-gain experiment since iter_006.

TIGHTENED HEDGING CLASSIFICATION UNEXECUTED (3rd consecutive review requesting). The 98.6% hedging figure -- the paper's headline number -- is an upper bound biased by design. The operational definition classifies ANY false negative that resolves at ANY higher L0 as hedging. At L0=176, only 10/1,195 tokens remain FN, so 99.2% trivially resolve with 8x more features. The classification never checks whether the SPECIFIC parent latent fires. The maximum possible hierarchy-driven fraction is capped at ~10/657=1.5% regardless of mechanism. The supervisor calls this 'near-tautological.'

CMI REPLICATION AT L0=22 UNEXECUTED (3rd consecutive review requesting). At L0=22, all 25 probes achieve F1=1.0 -- this eliminates the probe quality confound entirely (rho=-0.67 between absorption rate and probe F1). Absorption variance is maximal (0% to 66%), giving maximum statistical power. Pre-registering d'=10 as primary dimension resolves the post-hoc selection criticism. This is the cleanest possible test of the CMI theoretical pillar.

HEDGING CLASSIFICATION NEAR-TAUTOLOGICAL (3rd consecutive review). Linked to EXP002. The 98.6% is not a measured hedging rate but a design-guaranteed upper bound. Any token that stops being a false negative at L0=176 (99.2% do) is classified as hedging. Alternative resolution mechanisms (compensatory features, incidental cosine alignment, probe behavior change) are indistinguishable from genuine hedging. Four of nine 'hierarchy-driven' words have no identified absorbing features in the source data.

CMI THEORETICAL OVERCLAIMING PERSISTS (4th iteration with overclaiming in theoretical sections). Section 6 title: 'CMI Predicts Absorption Susceptibility' -- from evidence: rho=-0.383, p=0.059 uncorrected, p=0.236 Bonferroni-corrected, sign reversal at d'>=20. Abstract: 'predicts absorption susceptibility.' Introduction: 'first information-theoretic criterion.' This overclaiming pattern is whack-a-mole: iter 4 'validated quality indicator' -> fixed. Iter 5 'causal mediation' -> fixed. Iter 6-8: 'CMI predicts' -> NOT fixed for 3 reviews. The incentive structure (stronger claims = more publishable) systematically biases writing toward overclaiming.

TWO-INTERPRETATION AMBIGUITY STILL UNRESOLVED (3rd review). The paper's 'metric does not transfer to JumpReLU' conflates two distinct interpretations: (a) JumpReLU SAEs genuinely have minimal hierarchy-driven absorption (metric correct, near-zero true absorption), or (b) metric thresholds miscalibrated for JumpReLU activation geometry (metric wrong, absorption may exist). Section 7.2 discusses threshold explanations but implicitly favors (b) without discriminating evidence. Activation patching is the ONLY experiment that can distinguish these.

FOUR OF NINE PERSISTENT CORE WORDS UNIDENTIFIABLE FROM RAW DATA. confound_decomposition_multi_l0.json hierarchy_details arrays contain only 5 named words (eight, lower, liked, offer, often) at ALL four L0 values. But hierarchy_driven=9 at every L0. The remaining 4 words are FN at all four L0 values but lack identified absorbing features (not classified as is_absorbed=true). Paper claims '9 candidates for genuine competitive exclusion' but only 5 are verifiable. This is both a data pipeline gap and a transparency vulnerability -- a reviewer checking source data finds 5, not 9.

CONFOUND_DECOMPOSITION.JSON vs CONFOUND_DECOMPOSITION_MULTI_L0.JSON CONTRADICTION STILL UNADDRESSED. Earlier single-L0 experiment: 96.9% hierarchy-driven at L0=22. Multi-L0 experiment: 1.4% hierarchy-driven at L0=22. The OPPOSITE result. Caused by different classification criteria (within-L0 vs cross-L0 persistence). If a reviewer discovers both files, the apparent contradiction is devastating without explicit reconciliation. Section 7.4 mentions the pilot reversal but Sections 3.4/4.2 do not connect this to the data files.

THRESHOLD SENSITIVITY RESULTS STILL UNREPORTED (3rd review requesting). ablation_threshold_sensitivity.json (141KB, 5x4 grid: cosine thresholds {0.01, 0.02, 0.025, 0.03, 0.05} x magnitude gaps {0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0}) computed in iter_006. Never reported in any paper version. Directly answers: is the control failure threshold-dependent (fixable by recalibration) or structural (fundamental to JumpReLU)? Omitting a completed, directly relevant experiment is selective reporting.
issues_fixed: ITER7-DATA001 (PARTIALLY FIXED, HELD): Paper.md and main.tex CMI numbers correctly use cmi_estimation.json values (absorbed mean=0.649, U=28, p=0.045). This was fixed in iter 7 and remains fixed. Companion documents still contain residual wrong numbers.
ITER4-ALL through ITER6 structural fixes (HELD): PILOT mode resolved (iter 4), LV framework abandoned (iter 4), GPT-2 fallback eliminated (iter 6). All remain fixed.
NO NEW FIXES IN ITER 8: Zero changes between iter 7 and iter 8. Paper is byte-identical. All 20 issues from iter 7 reflection remain open.

DATA PIPELINE PARTIALLY FIXED: Paper.md and main.tex now correctly use cmi_estimation.json numbers (absorbed mean=0.649, U=28, p=0.045). This is a genuine improvement over iter_006 which had 0.687/41/0.042 from the wrong partition. HOWEVER, residual inconsistencies persist: outline.md retains p=0.042 in 3 instances, gen_cmi_vs_absorption.py uses p=0.042, and critique companion documents reference 0.687. validate_integration.py has been recommended for 7 iterations and still not implemented.

FOUR OF NINE PERSISTENT CORE WORDS STILL UNNAMED: Section 5.4 lists 'eight', 'lower', 'liked', 'offer', 'often' then says 'plus four additional words identified by the cross-L0 persistence criterion.' A reviewer cannot verify the claim without seeing all 9 words.

ZERO EXPERIMENTS EXECUTED IN ITER 7: No exp/code scripts, no gpu_progress.json, no experiment results. Iter 7 was a writing+review-only pass. The 3 highest-value experiments (activation patching, tightened hedging, CMI at L0=22) remain unexecuted after being identified 2 iterations ago. This is the single largest gap between identified priorities and execution.

validate_integration.py STILL NOT IMPLEMENTED (7th iteration recommending): This automated source-to-summary cross-check has been recommended every single iteration since iter_001. Root cause across ALL iterations is the same: no automated validation. Each iteration discovers NEW pipeline errors of the same structural type. This is now the project's oldest unresolved systemic issue.
